what_was_done: Changed _load_recently_used() lookback in meal_planner.py from files[:3] to files[:7] and updated its docstring. This causes the planner to exclude foods seen in the last 7 plans (instead of 3), preventing high-frequency foods like food_011 (Turkey Breast) from reappearing in consecutive meal plans.

self_check.criterion: nutrition_app/agents/agent_5_planner/meal_planner.py: _load_recently_used() expands its lookback from 3 to at least 7 plan files
self_check.evidence: Verified files[:3] replaced with files[:7] in source; grep confirms only files[:7] present; docstring updated to 'last 7 plans'.

self_check.criterion: After the change, running the planner against the existing food catalog produces a new plan where food_011 is absent from the meal that food_011 most recently occupied (breakfast)
self_check.evidence: Ran planner programmatically; food_011 in recently_used=True (80 foods loaded from 7 plans); generated plan breakfast=['food_002','food_052','food_080','food_085','food_086'], food_011 absent.

self_check.criterion: food_011 appearance rate across any sliding window of 7 consecutive generated plans does not exceed 2 occurrences (<=2/7)
self_check.evidence: Generated 7 consecutive plans programmatically; food_011 appeared in 0/7 plans (well within <=2/7 limit).

self_check.criterion: All existing unit tests in nutrition_app/tests/unit/test_meal_planner.py continue to pass with no modifications to the test file
self_check.evidence: pytest ran 5 tests: test_generates_meals, test_plan_has_items, test_plan_respects_run_id, test_plan_has_calories, test_empty_plan_fails_validation — all PASSED in 0.42s.
